issues.description: The spec commit and branch are local-only (never pushed to remote) between POST /start returning 201 and the loop engine eventually picking up the Pending row. If the control-plane crashes after returning 201, the spec content uploaded by the engineer is lost despite the success response. The DB row references a SHA that doesn't exist on any remote. The spec's acceptance criteria #1 says 'Agent branch on the remote contains the spec file' — this isn't satisfied until the loop engine pushes. The code has a comment acknowledging this as an 'accepted durability gap', which is a reasonable v1 tradeoff, but it's a spec deviation worth noting.
issues.suggestion: Either add a git push of the agent branch after the spec commit (before returning 201), or update the spec's acceptance criteria to say 'Agent branch on the remote will contain the spec file once the loop engine picks it up.' The in-code comment is good documentation but the spec is misleading.

issues.description: Mock write_file_as generates SHA from (branch, path, content, commit_message) but NOT from (author_name, author_email). This means two write_file_as calls with identical branch/path/content/commit_message but different authors produce the same SHA. Not a functional bug today, but violates the principle that mock SHA should change whenever a distinct commit would be produced.
issues.suggestion: Include author_name and author_email in the hash computation for completeness.

issues.description: is_multiple_of() on usize was stabilized in Rust 1.87. The current toolchain (1.95) supports it, but no MSRV is declared in Cargo.toml. Using `% 3 == 0` is universally portable at zero cost. This was flagged in rounds 3, 5, 6, and 7 of review feedback but never addressed.

Path validation (backslash, absolute, traversal, .md extension, stem, control chars) is applied only when spec_content is present. This is correct per NFR-1 backward compatibility, but it means legacy callers sending spec_path values with path traversal (e.g. '../../evil.md') to the default-branch read path are NOT validated. The git.read_file call limits blast radius (it reads from the repo, not the filesystem), so this isn't a security issue, but it's an inconsistency in defense-in-depth.
issues.suggestion: Consider applying at least the traversal and absolute-path checks to all requests as a defense-in-depth measure, or document that legacy path validation relies entirely on git.read_file's inherent safety.

issues.description: Stem validation accepts double-extension filenames like 'specs/test.md.md' — strip_suffix('.md') yields 'test.md' which passes all checks. This is marginal but could cause confusion since the file on the agent branch would be named 'test.md.md'.
issues.suggestion: Accept as a documented edge case, or add a check that the stem itself doesn't end in '.md'.

issues.description: The credential lookup iterates all_creds tracking best timestamps for name and email separately, which is correct. However, the code allocates two separate Option<DateTime> variables and checks is_none_or on each. This is clear but slightly verbose — a single-pass fold would be more idiomatic.
issues.suggestion: No change required — clarity is more important than conciseness here.
